text.title: Agent Reasoning Depth Budget Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Agent Reasoning Depth Budget application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Start at the model's minimum thinking budget (1-2k tokens). Do not start at max.
rule.rationale: Cost discipline: starting at max guarantees overspend; min surfaces under-thinking failures fast + cheap.

rule.statement: Double thinking budget until eval score stops improving (≤1pp gain over 3 doublings). Round up to nearest 1k for headroom.
rule.rationale: Empirical sizing > intuition; plateau is the only signal surviving model version changes.

rule.statement: If failures cluster on multi-step / constraint-heavy AND thinking-token avg < 25% of budget, raise minimum thinking floor.
rule.rationale: Symptom: agent skips planning step; raising floor (not ceiling) restores planning behavior.

rule.statement: If thinking-token P95 == budget AND eval gain over prior tier ≤1pp, reduce cap to plateau.
rule.rationale: Spending past plateau gains nothing measurable; trim to empirical knee.

rule.statement: Compress prior thinking into ≤200 tokens of state summary before next turn. Do not re-include full thinking traces.
rule.rationale: Re-including thinking inflates context 3-10× with negligible score benefit.
